auth_notes: Requires SMART on FHIR protocol implementation. This involves discovering endpoints via the .well-known/smart-configuration, handling specific FHIR scopes, and managing bearer tokens that are often tenant-specific.
rate_limits: Not explicitly defined globally; varies by tenant and application tier. Throttling is managed via 429 status codes.
gotchas: FHIR versioning differences (DSTU2 vs R4) across different Cerner implementations
Strict IP whitelisting and SSL requirements for production environments
Complex data mapping required to transform JSON responses into usable business objects
research_notes: Integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. Developers must first query the metadata/conformance statement to identify the OAuth2 endpoints. For system-to-system integration, the 'Client Credentials' flow is used, but for user-facing apps, the 'Authorization Code' flow is required. All data must be handled according to HIPAA compliance standards within the n8n environment.
